title: Inorder successor and predecessor in BST

content.problemStatement:
Given a Binary Search Tree and a ‘key’ value which represents the data data of a node in this tree. Return the inorder predecessor and successor of the given node in the BST.

The predecessor of a node in BST is that node that will be visited just before the given node in the inorder traversal of the tree. Return nullptr if the given node is the one that is visited first in the inorder traversal.
The successor of a node in BST is that node that will be visited immediately after the given node in the inorder traversal of the tree. Return nullptr if the given node is visited last in the inorder traversal.
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the BST <= 10^5
content.inputFormat: The input will contain a binary search tree (BST) and a node value. The BST will be represented as a tree where each node has a value and references to its left and right children. The node value will be an integer.
content.outputFormat: The output will be the inorder predecessor and successor of the given node in the BST. If the given node is the first or last node in the inorder traversal, the corresponding predecessor or successor will be null.

hidden.label: Tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: To find the inorder predecessor and successor, we can perform an inorder traversal of the BST and keep track of the previous and next nodes.

code.Python3:
class TreeNode:
    def __init__(self, x):
        self.val = x
        self.left = None
        self.right = None

# Function to find the inorder predecessor and successor
def find_predecessor_and_successor(root, p):
    predecessor = None
    successor = None
    stack = []
    current = root
    while current or stack:
        while current:
            stack.append(current)
            current = current.left
        current = stack.pop()
        if predecessor is None and current.val < p.val:
            predecessor = current
        if current == p:
            # Find the successor
            if current.right:
                temp = current.right
                while temp.left:
                    temp = temp.left
                successor = temp
        if successor is None and current.val > p.val:
            successor = current
            break
        current = current.right
    return predecessor, successor

# Create the BST
root = TreeNode(5)
root.left = TreeNode(3)
root.left.left = TreeNode(2)
root.left.right = TreeNode(4)
root.right = TreeNode(7)

# Find the inorder predecessor and successor
p = root.left
predecessor, successor = find_predecessor_and_successor(root, p)

# Print the result
print("Predecessor:", predecessor.val if predecessor else "NULL")
print("Successor:", successor.val if successor else "NULL")
